researchFindings: Canon check confirmed Andorhal as the grain-distribution hub feeding northern Lordaeron, including Stratholme and smaller plague routes.
Canon check confirmed Hearthglen as a fortified holdout in the Arthas/Uther timeline; the chapter's held-but-still-losing aftermath fits established geography and event order.
Canon check confirmed Stratholme as the key city-scale grain outbreak and the immediate pre-Culling target in the Third War sequence.
Canon check confirmed Arthas as crown prince/paladin and Uther as the Silver Hand's senior commander during this phase; title usage in the chapter is accurate.
Canon check confirmed Kel'Thuzad, Northrend, and the Lich King chain behind the plague; no lore correction was required in the draft.

changes.before: A child's shoe lay half sunk in the mud where the wheel-ruts deepened, leather dark with water, one lace worked down into the track by passing wagons.
changes.after: A child's shoe lay half sunk in the mud where the ruts deepened.

changes.before: Smoke still came out of farmhouse chimneys. Supper still mattered to the people going north.
changes.after: The domestic tag was cut so the road pivots faster into Hearthglen.

changes.before: The town stood where it had stood yesterday in reports and maps...
changes.after: Stone walls. Square towers. Chapel roof above the inner line.

changes.before: Bodies lay on doors lifted from hinges and on ladders stripped of their use. Two boys dragged straw toward the pyres. A horse screamed from somewhere behind the stables and went silent all at once.
changes.after: Bodies lay on doors pulled from hinges. Two boys dragged straw toward the pyres.

changes.before: Not calmer. Simply narrowed by people who had been at it too long to waste anything.
changes.after: Not calmer. Narrowed by people who had been at it too long to waste anything.

changes.before: Men were looking at the sacks as if they expected them to bite.
changes.after: The extra reaction beat was cut; the untouched grain already carries the threat.

changes.before: He had the look of a man who had not sat down since yesterday and intended to be praised for that later, if there was a later.
changes.after: He looked as if he had not sat since yesterday.

changes.before: The room around him was map boards, chalk, knives, relay strips, two tired officers, and the hard smell of wet wool.
changes.after: The room around him was maps, chalk, knives, relay strips, two tired officers.

changes.before: the countryside had been maintained to move plague and silence witnesses at once
changes.after: the countryside kept orderly enough to move plague and silence witnesses

changes.before: Her voice came out quick and built, every clause landing where she meant it.
changes.after: Her voice came out quick and built.

changes.before: Not warmth. Not agreement. Sharper attention.
Not the whole city. Specific feed points.
changes.after: Granaries if any stock is intact. Bakehouses.

changes.before: That one had not been common among them.
changes.after: That one was not common among them.

changes.before: The departure rush had already begun. Stable boys ran tack from chest to mounting line. Quartermasters shouted over one another with the fury of men who knew speed would be blamed on them if it failed.
changes.after: The departure rush had begun. Stable boys ran tack from chest to mounting line. Quartermasters shouted over one another.

changes.before: Jaina gathered the reins, eyes already on the lane opening through the camp.
changes.after: Jaina gathered the reins, eyes on the lane opening through the camp.

observations.note: Andorhal grain-hub canon matched the chapter's briefing language, so no route correction was needed.
Hearthglen's held-but-battered status matched Warcraft III chronology and the project's location notes.
Stratholme remained the right next-city pressure point; no geography drift surfaced.
Arthas/Uther title usage was accurate for this pre-Culling phase.
Kel'Thuzad -> Lich King -> Northrend escalation remained canon-grounded and needed no rewrite.
Cut the opening road paragraph down to one dominant image plus refugee pressure.
Compressed the Hearthglen reveal so the chapter reaches active aftermath faster.
Trimmed the triage-yard description by dropping redundant shock beats after the corpse rise.
Tightened the Uther walk-in by reducing repeated suffering inventory once the field pattern was clear.
Compressed the departure rush so the chapter exits the camp as soon as the head start is earned.
Removed one narrator-restatement fragment stack in the camp-motion paragraph.
Removed one extra reaction line around the untouched grain sacks; the object already carried the dread.
Cut Falric's self-aware tag from a longer explanatory clause to a sharper field read.
Trimmed Arthas-room inventory to stop the list from competing with his entrance.
Reduced attribution over-explanation in Jaina's briefing voice.
Removed one document-ish preamble from Jaina's source-plan pitch and started on the concrete targets.
Dropped one redundant silence-weight sentence before Arthas's lane decision.
Dropped one meta-summary sentence after the horseside exchange and let the action carry it.

Meta-audited the three densest v2 paragraphs: Sylvanas's road briefing, Jaina's shell explanation, and the departure rush. Kept them because each carries operational information, not decorative sludge.
Scanned every quoted line; no actionable/vector/optimal/ingested-style contamination remained.
Arthas's command lines stayed in live field-order register rather than drifting into memo language.
Jaina's technical explanation stayed inside mage voice: shell, body, wards, gate; no modern lab words appeared.
Uther's lines stayed plain, grave, and spoken; none of his moral pressure turned bureaucratic.
The brief horseside exchange was kept clipped so Sylvanas/Jaina sound operational rather than reconciliatory.
Re-read the briefing block, the source-plan block, and the final ride block word by word after trimming. The remaining density reads as pressure, not AI padding.

flags: Kept 'route packet, store maps, signal priority' in Arthas's command because the scene is a live command tent and the line reads as immediate field shorthand, not report prose.
Kept the fourth 'May the stars keep your road' sighting unchanged for the Mal'Ganis breadcrumb and Cyndia's recognition beat.
Kept Uther's 'conference' echo because it lands as irritation directed at Falric, not bureaucratic dialogue drift.
